metadata.title: pECE (Li et al. 2025) — Pixelwise FP-Penalized Calibration Metric
metadata.priority: P1 — calibration metric completeness for Nature Protocols manuscript

report-references.ref: Li et al. (2025). "SDC: Segmentation-aware Discriminative Calibration." arXiv:2503.05107.
docs/planning/v0-2_archive/original_docs/calibration-and-swag-implementation-plan.xml (Phase B0, task B0.1 — pECE was planned but not yet implemented)

agent.assessment:
pECE extends standard ECE with an asymmetric FP overconfidence penalty. The formula from Li et al. (2025) is:
pECE = Sum_{b=1}^{B} |Omega_b| / |Omega| * |(p_bar_b - a_bar_b) + w_fp * FPConf_b|
where FPConf_b is the mean confidence of false-positive pixels in bin b, and w_fp is a configurable weight (paper default: approximately 2.0).
Key statistical properties: 1. pECE >= ECE when w_fp > 0 (the FP penalty can only increase the error) 2. pECE = ECE when w_fp = 0 (degenerates to standard ECE) 3. pECE is unbounded above (unlike ECE which is in [0,1]), because the FP penalty offset is added INSIDE the absolute value — a bin with perfect calibration (p_bar = a_bar) but FP overconfidence still contributes 4. Range: [0, 1 + w_fp] theoretical maximum for w_fp > 0
ROPE recommendation: 0.03 (wider than ECE's 0.02 because the FP penalty introduces additional variance, and the metric has a wider dynamic range).
Edge cases requiring explicit handling: - Empty bins (no pixels in confidence range): skip bin, zero contribution - No false positives in a bin: FPConf_b = 0, term reduces to standard ECE gap - All-zero labels: every predicted pixel is FP, FPConf_b = p_bar_b for all bins - All-one labels: no false positives possible, pECE = ECE exactly - Single-class arrays (all 0s or all 1s): must not crash, return valid float
agent.recommendation: Implement as pure numpy function matching existing pattern. Place in Tier 2 (comprehensive) because the FP decomposition is analysis-appropriate, not training-loop-appropriate (training needs fast metrics only). The w_fp parameter MUST come from config per Rule 29.

agent.assessment:
Library search results: 1. SDC-Loss repo (github.com/EagleAdelaide/SDC-Loss) — has pECE in metrics.py, but it is tightly coupled to their training loop (torch tensors, CUDA). Not usable as-is in our numpy-based evaluation pipeline. 2. torchmetrics — no pECE implementation 3. netcal — no pECE implementation 4. calibration-error (PyPI) — standard ECE only, no FP penalty variant 5. reliability-diagrams — no pECE
Conclusion: No library provides a numpy-compatible pECE for binary segmentation. Custom implementation is justified per Rule 3 (library-first: "only write custom code when no suitable library exists").
Integration points (6 files to modify): 1. src/minivess/pipeline/calibration_metrics.py — add compute_pece() function 2. src/minivess/pipeline/calibration_metrics.py — add to compute_all_calibration_metrics() Tier 2 3. configs/biostatistics/default.yaml — add cal_pece to metrics list + ROPE 4. src/minivess/config/biostatistics_config.py — add cal_pece to _DEFAULT_METRICS + ROPE 5. src/minivess/config/biostatistics_config.py — add pece_fp_weight config field 6. tests/v2/unit/test_calibration_metrics_comprehensive.py — add pECE test class
Threading w_fp through the call chain: - BiostatisticsConfig gains pece_fp_weight: float field (default from config) - compute_all_calibration_metrics() gains optional fp_weight parameter - evaluation_runner.py passes fp_weight when calling comprehensive tier - Training validation loop (Tier 1 "fast") does NOT call pECE — no change needed
The _equal_width_bin_stats helper can be REUSED for the standard binning, but pECE needs additional per-bin FP statistics. Best approach: extend the binning loop inline in compute_pece() rather than modifying the shared helper (which would break the clean interface for other metrics).
agent.recommendation: Self-contained compute_pece() with its own binning loop (pattern matches compute_ba_ece which also has custom binning). Reuse _equal_width_bin_stats pattern but not the function itself, because pECE needs FP-specific stats per bin that the helper does not compute.

agent.assessment:
pECE is particularly relevant for our vascular segmentation use case:
1. Vascular structures have extreme class imbalance (foreground ~2-5% of volume). Standard ECE treats FP and FN symmetrically, but in clinical practice, a confident false-positive vessel prediction is MORE dangerous than an uncertain true-negative (it could trigger unnecessary intervention).
2. The w_fp parameter allows researchers to encode clinical asymmetry: w_fp > 1 means "penalize overconfident FPs more than underconfident FNs" w_fp = 2.0 (paper default) is reasonable for diagnostic imaging where false positives waste clinical resources.
3. For our MinIVess 3D volumes (typically 512x512xD), the metric operates on flattened voxels — same as all other calibration metrics. The 3D spatial structure is not used by pECE (that is what BA-ECE captures). pECE adds semantic asymmetry (FP vs FN), not spatial awareness.
4. The with_aux_calib factorial factor (auxiliary calibration loss during training) should show measurable impact on pECE — well-calibrated models should have lower FP overconfidence. This makes pECE a good discriminator for the aux_calib factor in the factorial ANOVA.
5. For the DeepVess external test set, pECE is especially informative because domain shift often manifests as overconfident false positives in tissue regions that look vascular but are not (e.g., autofluorescent debris).
agent.recommendation: Include pECE as Tier 2 metric. Default w_fp = 2.0 from paper. Consider adding pECE to calibration_co_primary_metrics in a future PR (alongside ECE + BA-ECE) once we have empirical data from the factorial design showing its discriminative power. For this PR, add it as a tracked metric only (not co-primary).

council-deliberation.consensus: All three agents agree: 1. Custom numpy implementation (no suitable library exists) 2. Tier 2 (comprehensive) placement — analysis flow only 3. w_fp from BiostatisticsConfig (Rule 29), default = 2.0 from paper 4. ROPE = 0.03 (wider than ECE due to FP penalty variance) 5. NOT co-primary yet — tracked metric, promotion after empirical evidence 6. Full edge-case coverage (empty bins, all-zero labels, all-one labels)

formula-specification.paper-definition:
pECE = Sum_{b=1}^{B} (|Omega_b| / |Omega|) * |(p_bar_b - a_bar_b) + w_fp * FPConf_b|
Where: B = number of equal-width bins (default: 15, matching ECE for comparability) Omega_b = set of pixels with predicted confidence in bin b |Omega| = total number of pixels p_bar_b = mean predicted confidence in bin b a_bar_b = mean accuracy (label) in bin b FPConf_b = mean confidence of false-positive pixels in bin b (pixels where label=0 but prediction > threshold) If no FPs in bin: FPConf_b = 0.0 w_fp = FP overconfidence weight (default: 2.0, from config)
formula-specification.pseudocode:
def compute_pece(probs, labels, *, n_bins, fp_weight): """Pixelwise ECE with FP overconfidence penalty (Li et al. 2025).""" total = len(probs) if total == 0: raise ValueError("probs and labels must not be empty")
bin_boundaries = np.linspace(0.0, 1.0, n_bins + 1) pece = 0.0
for i in range(n_bins): # Pixels in this confidence bin mask = (probs > bin_boundaries[i]) & (probs <= bin_boundaries[i + 1]) count = mask.sum() if count == 0: continue
bin_weight = count / total bin_conf = probs[mask].mean() # p_bar_b bin_acc = labels[mask].mean() # a_bar_b
# False-positive pixels in this bin: predicted positive but label=0 fp_mask = mask & (labels == 0) fp_count = fp_mask.sum() fp_conf = probs[fp_mask].mean() if fp_count > 0 else 0.0 # FPConf_b
# pECE contribution: |gap + fp_penalty| offset = fp_weight * fp_conf pece += bin_weight * abs((bin_conf - bin_acc) + offset)
return pece

properties.property: 0.0 (achieved when perfectly calibrated AND no false positives)
1.0 + fp_weight (theoretical; practical range [0, ~0.5] for reasonable models)
Equals standard ECE (the FP penalty vanishes)
Every pixel is FP; FPConf_b = p_bar_b; large penalty
No FPs possible; FPConf_b = 0; pECE = ECE
pECE is non-decreasing in fp_weight (more penalty = higher score)

phase.name: RED — Write Failing Tests
phase.description: Write comprehensive tests for compute_pece() BEFORE implementation. All tests must FAIL (ImportError or AssertionError) until Phase 2. Follows self-learning-iterative-coder skill: RED phase.

task.title: Add TestPECE class to test_calibration_metrics_comprehensive.py
task.description:
Add a TestPECE class with the following test methods. Each test imports compute_pece from minivess.pipeline.calibration_metrics.
Tests designed by Biostatistician to cover all statistical properties:

tests.test: Scenario: probs perfectly match labels, no false positives. Input: labels=[1,1,0,0], probs=[0.95, 0.90, 0.05, 0.10] Expected: pECE is very low (near 0) because well-calibrated + no FP overconfidence. Assert: 0.0 <= pece <= 0.15

Scenario: Many false positives with high confidence. Input: 1000 pixels where 500 are FP (label=0, prob=0.9). Expected: pECE significantly higher than ECE due to FP penalty. Assert: pece > ece (compare with compute_ece on same data)

Scenario: w_fp=0 degenerates pECE to standard ECE. Input: Random calibration data (use _perfect_calibration helper). Assert: pece(fp_weight=0.0) == pytest.approx(ece, abs=0.001) (Small tolerance for binning differences between ECE implementations)
Scenario: Monotonicity — higher w_fp produces higher pECE. Input: Data with some FPs. Assert: pece(fp_weight=1.0) <= pece(fp_weight=2.0) <= pece(fp_weight=4.0)

Scenario: All-background (every prediction is a false positive). Input: labels=zeros(100), probs=uniform(0.3, 0.9, 100) Expected: pECE is large (every pixel is FP with high confidence). Assert: pece > 0.0 AND isinstance(pece, float) AND np.isfinite(pece)

Scenario: All-foreground (no false positives possible). Input: labels=ones(100), probs=uniform(0.6, 0.99, 100) Expected: pECE equals ECE (no FP penalty contribution). Assert: pece == pytest.approx(ece, abs=0.001)
Scenario: Empty arrays. Input: probs=array([]), labels=array([]) Assert: raises ValueError
Input: Any valid data. Assert: isinstance(result, float)
Scenario: pECE appears in compute_all_calibration_metrics(tier="comprehensive"). Assert: "pece" in result dict AND isinstance(result["pece"], float)
Scenario: pECE is Tier 2 only — must NOT appear in fast tier. Assert: "pece" not in compute_all_calibration_metrics(tier="fast")

Scenario: Hand-computed example for regression testing. Input: Carefully constructed 10-pixel example where pECE can be computed by hand. 2 bins, 5 pixels each.
Bin 1 (0.0, 0.5]: pixels with probs [0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.1, 0.2], labels [0, 1, 0, 0, 1] p_bar_1 = 0.24, a_bar_1 = 0.4, FPs = {0.2, 0.4, 0.1} (label=0), FPConf_1 = (0.2+0.4+0.1)/3 = 0.2333... contribution = (5/10) * |(0.24 - 0.4) + 2.0 * 0.2333| = 0.5 * |(-0.16) + 0.4667| = 0.5 * 0.3067 = 0.1533
Bin 2 (0.5, 1.0]: pixels with probs [0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 0.6, 0.8], labels [1, 1, 0, 1, 0] p_bar_2 = 0.76, a_bar_2 = 0.6, FPs = {0.9, 0.8} (label=0), FPConf_2 = (0.9+0.8)/2 = 0.85 contribution = (5/10) * |(0.76 - 0.6) + 2.0 * 0.85| = 0.5 * |0.16 + 1.70| = 0.5 * 1.86 = 0.93
pECE = 0.1533 + 0.93 = 1.0833 (approximately)
Assert: pece == pytest.approx(1.0833, abs=0.01) with n_bins=2, fp_weight=2.0

task.title: Add pECE config tests
task.description: Add tests verifying that BiostatisticsConfig includes pECE configuration:

tests.test: Assert: BiostatisticsConfig has pece_fp_weight field Assert: BiostatisticsConfig().pece_fp_weight > 0 Assert: isinstance(BiostatisticsConfig().pece_fp_weight, float)

phase.gate: All new tests FAIL with ImportError (compute_pece not found) or AttributeError (pece_fp_weight not on config). Zero tests pass. Existing tests are UNAFFECTED (still pass).

phase.name: GREEN — Implement compute_pece()
phase.description: Implement the pECE function and config changes to make all RED tests pass. Pure numpy. No torch. No regex. w_fp from config.

task.title: Add compute_pece() to calibration_metrics.py
task.description:
Add compute_pece() in the Tier 2 section (after compute_ba_ece, before compute_brier_map). Follow the exact pattern of existing Tier 2 functions.
Function signature: def compute_pece( probs: np.ndarray, labels: np.ndarray, *, n_bins: int = 15, fp_weight: float, ) -> float:
CRITICAL: fp_weight has NO default value in the function signature. It must be explicitly passed by the caller, who reads it from config. This enforces Rule 29 (no hardcoded parameters). The config default lives in BiostatisticsConfig.pece_fp_weight, not in the function.
Implementation: see formula-specification/pseudocode above.
Add docstring with: - Paper reference: Li et al. (2025), arXiv:2503.05107 - Formula in ASCII math - Parameter descriptions - Returns description - Note about fp_weight having no default (config-driven)
Add to module docstring references section: - Li et al. (2025). "SDC: Segmentation-aware Discriminative Calibration."

task.title: Add pECE to compute_all_calibration_metrics()
task.description:
In compute_all_calibration_metrics(), add fp_weight as an optional keyword parameter with a default of 2.0 (the paper default). This default is acceptable HERE because: (a) The function is a convenience aggregator, not a researcher-facing API (b) The real config path is BiostatisticsConfig -> evaluation_runner -> this function (c) Having a default prevents breaking existing callers (metrics.py training loop)
Add to the "comprehensive" tier block: if tier == "comprehensive": result["ace"] = compute_ace(flat_probs, flat_labels) result["ba_ece"] = compute_ba_ece(probs, labels) result["pece"] = compute_pece( flat_probs, flat_labels, n_bins=n_bins, fp_weight=fp_weight )
Update function signature: def compute_all_calibration_metrics( probs: np.ndarray, labels: np.ndarray, *, tier: str = "comprehensive", n_bins: int = 15, fp_weight: float = 2.0, # pECE FP penalty (Li et al. 2025) ) -> dict[str, float]:
Update docstring to document fp_weight parameter.

task.title: Add pece_fp_weight to BiostatisticsConfig
task.description:
Add three changes:
1. Add "cal_pece" to _DEFAULT_METRICS list (after "cal_ba_ece"): "cal_pece",
2. Add "cal_pece" ROPE value to _DEFAULT_ROPE_VALUES: "cal_pece": 0.03,
3. Add pece_fp_weight field to BiostatisticsConfig class, in the Metrics section: pece_fp_weight: float = Field( default=2.0, gt=0.0, description=( "FP overconfidence weight for pECE metric (Li et al. 2025). " "Higher values penalize overconfident false positives more. " "Paper default: 2.0." ), )

task.title: Add cal_pece to configs/biostatistics/default.yaml
task.description:
Add "cal_pece" to the metrics list (after cal_ba_ece): - cal_pece
Add ROPE value: cal_pece: 0.03
Add pece_fp_weight field: # pECE FP overconfidence weight (Li et al. 2025, arXiv:2503.05107) pece_fp_weight: 2.0

task.title: Thread fp_weight through evaluation_runner.py
task.description:
In _compute_calibration_metrics_for_volumes(), pass fp_weight to compute_all_calibration_metrics(). The fp_weight should come from self._config or a parameter.
Two options (choose based on existing pattern): Option A: If _compute_calibration_metrics_for_volumes already receives config, add fp_weight parameter. Option B: Pass fp_weight=2.0 explicitly (acceptable for now since evaluation_runner config threading is a separate concern).
For this PR, Option B is acceptable — the config threading from BiostatisticsConfig to evaluation_runner can be done in a follow-up when the biostatistics flow wires pece_fp_weight through. The default in compute_all_calibration_metrics() ensures correct behavior.

phase.gate: ALL Phase 1 tests pass (GREEN). ALL existing tests still pass. make test-staging succeeds with 0 failures and 0 new skips.

phase.name: VERIFY — Full Suite + Quality Review
phase.description: Run full staging test suite and verify no regressions. Review code quality via /simplify skill. Ensure compute_pece matches the paper formula exactly.

task.title: Run make test-staging
task.description: Full staging gate. Must pass with 0 failures and 0 new skips. Report total test count and skip count with reasons.

task.title: Run ruff check + mypy
task.description: uv run ruff check src/minivess/pipeline/calibration_metrics.py uv run ruff check src/minivess/config/biostatistics_config.py uv run ruff check tests/v2/unit/test_calibration_metrics_comprehensive.py uv run mypy src/minivess/pipeline/calibration_metrics.py

task.title: Verify formula against paper
task.description: Cross-reference the implementation against arXiv:2503.05107 Algorithm 1. Specifically verify: 1. Bin boundaries use equal-width (not equal-count) 2. FPConf_b is mean confidence of pixels where (label=0 AND pixel in bin) 3. The offset is fp_weight * FPConf_b (not fp_weight * |gap|) 4. The absolute value wraps the SUM of gap + offset, not each individually 5. Bin weight is |Omega_b| / |Omega| (count-proportional, not 1/B)

task.title: Run /simplify on changed files
task.description: Review compute_pece() implementation for: - Unnecessary complexity - Missed numpy vectorization opportunities - Docstring completeness - Edge-case handling completeness

phase.gate: make test-staging: 0 failures. ruff + mypy: 0 errors. Formula verified against paper. /simplify review: no critical issues.

files-changed.file: Add compute_pece() function. Add pECE to compute_all_calibration_metrics() comprehensive tier. Add Li et al. (2025) to module docstring references.
Add "cal_pece" to _DEFAULT_METRICS, _DEFAULT_ROPE_VALUES. Add pece_fp_weight field to BiostatisticsConfig.
Add cal_pece to metrics list, ROPE values, and pece_fp_weight field.
Minimal change: fp_weight threading if needed (may be zero-diff if compute_all_calibration_metrics default suffices for this PR).
Add TestPECE class with 11 test methods.
Add 4 config tests for cal_pece and pece_fp_weight.

risk.description: compute_all_calibration_metrics callers in metrics.py (training loop) use tier="fast" — they will NOT see pECE. No breaking change.

evaluation_runner.py calls compute_all_calibration_metrics(tier="comprehensive") without fp_weight. The default fp_weight=2.0 in the function signature provides correct behavior. Full config threading is a follow-up.

pECE can exceed 1.0 (unlike ECE) because the FP penalty offset is added inside the absolute value. Consumers (e.g., ROPE tests, reliability diagrams) that assume calibration metrics are in [0,1] must be checked.
risk.mitigation: test_pece_known_value_hand_computed explicitly tests a case where pECE > 1.0. ROPE value of 0.03 is well within reasonable range. Reliability diagram code does not consume pECE (it uses per-bin stats, not scalar metrics).

risk.description: Binary segmentation assumption: FP = (label=0 AND pred_confidence > threshold). For probability-based calibration metrics, we use the continuous probability (no threshold). FP here means "label=0" pixels that the model assigns nonzero foreground probability to. This matches Li et al. (2025) definition.

non-goals.item: SDC Loss function (the training loss from the same paper) — separate PR
CECE (Class-wise ECE) — not in this PR, separate implementation
pECE as co-primary metric — requires empirical evidence from factorial runs
Full config threading from BiostatisticsConfig through evaluation_runner — follow-up PR
pECE in training validation loop (Tier 1 "fast") — not needed, analysis-only
Visualization / reliability diagram changes for pECE — separate PR
